utility_details.utility_summary: Both wi_skills and wo_skills produced competent diagnoses that passed all verifier checks. However, wi_skills is more effective on the key differentiating criterion J4 (Adlerian task separation applied to family/partner pressure). The skill-equipped agent explicitly invoked the Adlerian framework, identified Signal C (direction-jumping) and Signal F (externalized blame) by name, and framed the family pressure episode as a psychological cover rather than legitimate strategic input — applying the correct conceptual lens. The wo_skills agent, while producing a longer and practically detailed report, treated parental advice as merely an 'external validation dependency' without applying task separation. Both agents identified the pivot pattern (J1), distinguished genuine from chosen constraints (J2), named the avoided action (J3), issued a conditional prognosis (J5), and prescribed concrete behavioral actions (J6). The primary advantage of wi_skills lies in the Adlerian framework application (J4) and the explicit use of diagnostic signal taxonomy (Signals A, C, F), which aligns more precisely with the expected diagnostic methodology. wo_skills compensates with more granular practical recommendations but lacks the theoretical precision the skill was designed to provide.
